next_steps: Obtain full autoimmune workup including RF, anti-CCP, ANA, ANCA, C3/C4 levels, and cryoglobulins
Consult rheumatology for formal evaluation of suspected rheumatoid vasculitis
Perform diagnostic sural nerve biopsy with concurrent gastrocnemius muscle biopsy
Complete formal MRI of the spine to rule out compressive myelopathy
Continue aggressive pain management and vitamin B6 replacement therapy
Monitor for development of systemic vasculitic manifestations (e.g., skin, renal, pulmonary)
Order inflammatory markers (ESR and CRP) to assess systemic inflammation
Perform comprehensive autoimmune panel (RF, anti-CCP, ANA, ANCA, C3/C4, cryoglobulins)
Consult rheumatology for formal evaluation of joint disease and potential vasculitis
Obtain age-appropriate malignancy screening (CT chest/abdomen/pelvis) given constitutional symptoms and multi-system decline
Perform diagnostic sural nerve and gastrocnemius muscle biopsy
Review nutritional history and evaluate for underlying causes of profound vitamin B6 deficiency (e.g., malabsorption)
Complete formal MRI of the spine with anesthesia if necessary to rule out structural contributors
